title: 10 Herbal Teas Proven to Help with Inflamed Gums
intro_desc: Herbal teas for inflamed gums are natural remedies that soothe and calm inflamed gum tissues, providing relief from pain, swelling, and discomfort. These teas work by reducing inflammation, fighting infection, and promoting healing. The best herbal teas for inflamed gums include Salvia officinalis, Echinacea purpurea, Achillea millefolium, Eucalyptus globulus, Melissa officinalis, Sanguinaria canadensis, Calendula officinalis, Ginkgo biloba, Mentha x piperita, and Hydrastis canadensis. If left untreated, inflamed gums can lead to difficulty eating, speaking, and even social isolation due to embarrassment and pain.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tea is a natural remedy that can help with inflamed gums. This type of inflammation, also known as gingivitis, is often caused by bacteria in the mouth that irritate the gums. Echinacea purpurea is a plant with powerful anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the gums. When made into a tea, the active compounds in the plant can be easily absorbed by the body, helping to combat the underlying causes of the inflammation. The anti-inflammatory effects of Echinacea purpurea tea can also help to reduce the redness and tenderness associated with inflamed gums. Additionally, the tea may help to boost the immune system, which can aid in fighting off the bacterial infection that causes the inflammation in the first place. Drinking Echinacea purpurea tea may also help to reduce the production of prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that contribute to inflammation. As a result, the tea can provide relief from inflamed gums and help to promote a healthy mouth.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for inflamed gums because it has strong anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and ease pain.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a soothing Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal tea that helps with inflamed gums, start by gathering dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots, also known as licorice root. Rinse the roots under cold water to remove any impurities. Next, in a saucepan, combine 1 teaspoon of the dried roots with 1 cup of boiling water. Reduce the heat to low and let the mixture simmer for 5-7 minutes, allowing the roots to release their natural sweetness and medicinal properties. Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the roots, then discard the solids. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but be sure to use it in moderation as Glycyrrhiza glabra can be quite sweet. Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate inflamed gums and promote oral health.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis tea is a natural remedy that can help with inflamed gums. The tea contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and pain in the gums. This is because the compounds in the tea, such as carnosic acid and ursolic acid, have the ability to block the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. When these enzymes are blocked, the inflammation in the gums is reduced, and the gums begin to heal. Additionally, the antibacterial properties of the tea can help to prevent infections in the gums, which can cause further inflammation. The tea also has antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the gums from damage caused by free radicals. This can help to promote healthy gums and prevent further inflammation.

remedies.image_alt: Salvia officinalis herbal teas for inflamed gums
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a soothing Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal tea for inflamed gums, start by gathering dried roots or root powder of the licorice plant, also known as Glycyrrhiza glabra. Begin by boiling 1 cup of water in a kettle or pot. While the water is heating up, take 1-2 teaspoons of the dried root powder or 1-2 dried roots, and add them to a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup. Once the water has reached a boil, carefully pour it over the licorice root, and let it steep for 5-10 minutes. You can also add a sweetener like honey if you prefer your tea sweeter. After the steeping time, carefully remove the tea infuser or roots, and strain the tea into another cup if needed. Let the tea cool down before drinking it. Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe inflamed gums and promote healthy oral health.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus tea is a popular remedy for inflamed gums. This is because eucalyptus contains compounds called flavonoids and tannins, which have strong antibacterial properties. These properties help reduce the number of bacteria in the mouth, which can cause inflammation and infection. Eucalyptus globulus tea also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm inflamed gums. When you drink eucalyptus globulus tea, the flavonoids and tannins are absorbed into your body and travel to your gums, where they can start to work their magic. The anti-inflammatory properties in eucalyptus globulus tea can help reduce swelling and ease pain in inflamed gums. Additionally, eucalyptus globulus tea has antiseptic properties, which can help prevent infection from spreading. This can help your gums heal faster and reduce the risk of further inflammation.

You can find it in health food stores or online. Boil 1 cup of water and then add the dried licorice root to it. Reduce the heat to a simmer and let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the root's active compounds to infuse into the water. Strain the tea into a cup to remove the licorice root. You can add honey to taste, as licorice root tea can be slightly bitter. Drinking this tea regularly may help reduce inflammation and soothe inflamed gums, thanks to its anti-inflammatory properties. It's also essential to note that excessive consumption of licorice root can be harmful, so moderation is key.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita tea, also known as peppermint tea, is good for inflamed gums because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The main ingredient, menthol, helps to reduce swelling and pain in the gums. When you drink peppermint tea, the menthol gets into your bloodstream and reaches your gums, where it starts to work its magic. It numbs the area, making it less painful to eat or brush your teeth. Peppermint tea also has antibacterial properties, which help to fight off the bacteria that can cause gum inflammation. Additionally, peppermint tea has a calming effect on the mouth and can help to reduce bad breath. The tea is also easy to make and can be a soothing addition to your oral care routine.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia tea, also known as tea tree oil, has been used for a long time to help with gum problems. The main reason for its effectiveness is that it has antibacterial properties, which means it can kill the germs that cause gum infections. When we eat, tiny bits of food get stuck in our teeth and between them. If these bits are left there, they can attract germs that cause plaque, which leads to inflammation and gum problems. Melaleuca alternifolia tea can help kill these germs, reducing the risk of infections and gum inflammation. Additionally, it has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and pain in the gums. This makes it a good solution for people who have inflamed gums due to infections or gum disease. The tea tree oil in Melaleuca alternifolia tea also has a soothing effect on the gums, which can help to calm and reduce inflammation. When used as a mouthwash or applied directly to the gums, it can be very effective in reducing gum inflammation and preventing further infections.

supplementary_best: For inflamed gums, the best combination of herbal teas is a blend of 'Melaleuca alternifolia', 'Melissa officinalis', and 'Glycyrrhiza glabra'. 'Melaleuca alternifolia', also known as tea tree oil, has antimicrobial properties that help reduce bacteria causing the inflammation. 'Melissa officinalis', or lemon balm, has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the gums and reduce swelling. 'Glycyrrhiza glabra', or licorice root, has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help heal the gums and reduce pain. This combination works well together because 'Melaleuca alternifolia' and 'Melissa officinalis' target the bacteria and inflammation, while 'Glycyrrhiza glabra' helps heal the gums. Drinking a tea made from these three herbs can provide relief from inflamed gums, reduce the risk of infection, and promote healing.
supplementary_avoid: If you have inflamed gums, there are some herbal teas you should avoid. One of these is Yarrow tea, made from the flowers of Achillea filipendula. Yarrow contains a compound called coumarin, which can irritate the gums and make bleeding worse.  Another tea to avoid is Red Clover tea, made from the flowers of Trifolium pratense. Red Clover contains isoflavones, which can increase the risk of bleeding and worsen gum inflammation.  You should also avoid Tea Tree substitute, Cinnamon tea, made from the bark of Cinnamomum verum. Cinnamon can irritate the gums and make them more sensitive.  Ginger substitute, Grains of Paradise tea, made from the rhizomes of Aframomum melegueta, should also be avoided. Grains of Paradise contain compounds that can irritate the gums and make inflammation worse.  Lastly, avoid Licorice Root substitute, Dandelion tea, made from the roots of Taraxacum officinale. Dandelion can cause inflammation and worsen gum problems.  These teas can be too harsh for inflamed gums, so it's best to choose gentler options.
supplementary_other_remedies: Aloe vera gel can help soothe inflamed gums because it has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and ease pain. When applied directly to the gums, aloe vera gel can calm irritated tissues and promote healing. Aloe vera also contains vitamins A, C, and E, which are antioxidants that fight off bacteria and other germs that can cause gum inflammation.  Another remedy is oil pulling with coconut oil, which involves swishing the oil around in your mouth for a few minutes before spitting it out. Coconut oil has antimicrobial properties that help kill bacteria in the mouth, reducing inflammation and preventing gum disease. Coconut oil also contains lauric acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated gums.  Saltwater rinse is also effective in reducing gum inflammation. When you rinse your mouth with warm saltwater, the salt helps reduce swelling and kill bacteria that can cause gum disease. Saltwater rinse also helps loosen and remove plaque and food particles that can get stuck in your teeth and irritate your gums.  Turmeric paste can also be applied directly to the inflamed gums to reduce swelling and ease pain. Turmeric contains curcumin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and calm irritated tissues. When applied to the gums, turmeric paste can help reduce inflammation and promote healing.  Dietary changes, such as eating foods rich in omega-3 fatty acids like salmon and flaxseeds, can also help reduce gum inflammation. Omega-3 fatty acids have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums. Foods rich in vitamin C, such as citrus fruits and leafy greens, can also help promote healing and reduce gum inflammation.  Epsom salt baths can also be beneficial for reducing gum inflammation. Epsom salt contains magnesium, a mineral that has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums. When you soak your face in warm water with Epsom salt, the magnesium can help relax and calm your muscles, including those in your gums.
